question: Which phrase means 'to praise someone'?
My teacher _____ always shouting at students last year. It was very stressful.
Which adjective means 'full of sympathy and care for others who are suffering'?
Read: 'A nurse called the baby Oliver Twist and he was sent to a house for orphans until he was nine.' Why was Oliver sent to a house for orphans?
choices: because his mother died after giving birth
because he ran away from home
because his father was in prison

question: When I was a child, we _____ go to the park every weekend. (repeated past action)
Which phrase means 'to admire or revere someone very greatly'?
Rewrite: 'What's the name of that website that sells retro-tech?' (Using participle clause)
choices: What's the name of that website sold retro-tech?
What's the name of that website to sell retro-tech?
What's the name of that website selling retro-tech?
What's the name of that website which selling retro-tech?

question: Which phrase means 'to quarrel and stop being friends with someone'?
Read: 'But a man, Mr Brownlow, told the police officer that Oliver had not taken anything.' Why did Mr Brownlow defend Oliver?
choices: because Mr Brownlow believed Oliver was innocent
because Mr Brownlow wanted to keep Oliver as a servant

question: In a defining relative clause, we _____ use 'that' instead of 'which' or 'who'.
Which adjective describes someone who 'genuinely means what they say'?
I _____ believe in ghosts, but not anymore. (past state that has ended)
What is the function of the INTRODUCTION in an opinion essay?
choices: to summarise the main points and restate the opinion
to give supporting arguments with examples
to summarise the topic and state the writer's position
to list facts and statistics only

question: The book _____ I read yesterday was very interesting.
Which phrase means 'to say things in order to annoy somebody'?
She is _____ dedicated to her work. She never stops.
Read: 'Around 97% of these children spend up to 6 hours a day online.' This is an example of:
choices: a fact that can be verified

question: When he was little, my brother used to _____ things out of proportion.
Which phrase means 'to deeply respect and greatly value someone as a role model'?
My grandfather's house, _____ I love, isn't very modern. (non-defining)
She _____ always complaining about the weather. (annoying habit in the present)
Which adjective describes someone who 'has a magnetic personality that attracts people'?
Read: 'The boys gathered together to give the old man watches and jewellery.' What does this tell us about Fagin?
choices: Fagin was teaching the boys to make watches
the boys were buying gifts for Fagin
the boys were bringing Fagin stolen goods
Fagin was giving the boys presents

question: When the relative pronoun is the _____ of the relative clause, you can omit it.
Which phrase means 'to help resolve an argument between others'?
If you want to be sure a secret is kept, tell Randa. She's _____.
In 'More often than not, young people communicate using messaging apps', this phrase means:
What does 'living day-to-day' mean?
choices: dealing with things without thinking about the future
travelling to a new place each day

question: Users _____ care about photograph quality will be happy with the new camera.
Read: 'I would say that while society may have changed, families remain as close as they were.' This sentence is used to:
choices: restate the thesis in the conclusion

question: Which word means 'to ask strangers for money or food'?
My brother _____ constantly losing his keys. He's so forgetful!
Which phrase means 'to have great admiration for someone, treating them like a role model'?
Read: 'Oliver was sad to leave the other orphans. Although the boys lived hand-to-mouth, they had a sense of belonging.' Why was Oliver sad to leave?
choices: because he had plenty of food there
because the orphans helped and looked after each other
because it was where he was born
because the workhouse was far from London

question: In an opinion essay, the MAIN BODY should include:
choices: two or three paragraphs with arguments including the given topics and your own idea

question: Which word means 'saying one thing but doing the opposite'?
She _____ turn up at 11 o'clock and act as if nothing's wrong. (typical present behaviour)
Which adjective describes someone who is 'very committed and works extremely hard for a cause'?
Televisions used to have a dial. The dial was used to change the channel. (Combine using relative clause)
choices: Televisions used to have a dial that was used to change the channel.
Televisions used to have a dial who was used to change the channel.
Televisions used to have a dial where was used to change the channel.
Televisions used to have a dial whose was used to change the channel.

question: What does 'a housekeeper' mean?
choices: a child whose parents have died
a person whose job is to look after a house
a person who works in a factory
a person who teaches children at home
